item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is assumed to be non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the bus-component of the image cannot be sourced. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has that special kind of horror that can't be properly explained. I like it, I liked the other two as well, man Eskobar, if you have anymore on these then I would be happy to read up.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Goddammit I thought this was serious.
Then I saw your name and showed the block.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer stopped. Author has vetoed edits.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ show block
– hide block

I propose the following addition:

31/05/09: Iapetus collides with Titan around the area where SCP-1480 is located. However, SCP-1480 appears unharmed from the crash, and messages flashed from the windows such as "i think i just got whiplash", "what did we hit" and "that's why seat-belts are important."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know this is a necro, but I don't believe the SCPS Ockham is a manned spacecraft. I think it's just a modified ESA probe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"the sender" Vincent Sender

This reminds me of "The Stupids" where the stupid idiots think "Return to Sender" on a piece of mail means there's an actual Mr. Sender stealing mail or whatever it was.

All in all, I don't get these three articles. The other two hit the perfect amount of creepy, except for this one. If there's a link between V. Sender and this "sender", I'm not seeing it other than tenuously, and even then, I feel this would be at least 100 times creepier without any discernible explanation of/or "source" of the phenomenon at all

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do they really need to spend the trillion or so dollars needed to send a crew to sit and watch a bus do nothing on Titan? Couldn't a probe do that just fine, allowing the ESA (Really? The ESA?) to use its big fancy spacecraft for better things like anything?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very interesting. Very neat. Well done on all three articles, Esko.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Some aspects of this entry mirror those of my own, SCP-1382. Was that intentional? It might be interesting if they were related.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sounds like they may need a driver upgrade.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Based on my knowledge of Eskobar as a writer, I don't think this is about what you might be referring to…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know how when you unplug your Internet router and plug it back in, one set of lights blinks for a second and then stays on, then the next one blinks and stays on, etc.? The first light is the windshield, and it goes all the way back. They communicate via light, and the driver turns their words into white noise, then silences them entirely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just noticed the significance of 'sender'. I thought it was strange word, but now it makes perfect sense. Sender. What'sat sound like? And what's one of the few named people in this? A Vincent Slender. The subject is children..

I want to upvote this even more now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well don't worry, we'll see the bus again when Iapetus knocks Titan into Earth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The picture kind of bugs me because the bus doesn't seem to be in perspective with the landscape, but it's not bad.

The second half of 13/01/05 is hard to follow and I'm not sure what happened, but I could tell it was something bad.

Definitely gave me the heebie jeebies. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is surprisingly creepy. "who is sender?" And then "they are not yours."

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It has been a while since I commissioned it, but I'm pretty sure I owe thanks to Faminepulse for the picture.
